description_long: This house features a traditional two-story design with a prominent gable roof covered in reddish-orange clay tiles. The exterior walls are finished in white plaster with dark wooden beams, giving it a classic half-timbered appearance. The front facade includes a small, gabled entryway with a wooden door and a tiled roof, flanked by symmetrical windows with crosshatched muntins. Two dormer windows protrude from the main roof, adding architectural interest and likely providing light to an attic or upper floor. The side elevations show additional windows and the continuation of the timber framing. The overall structure suggests a sturdy, residential building typical of European or Tudor-inspired urban neighborhoods.
description: A two-story, half-timbered house with a red-tiled gable roof, symmetrical windows, and a small gabled entryway. The design is traditional and visually detailed, with dormer windows and wooden accents.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with a gabled roof, two stories of windows, and two dormer windows on the tiled roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the gable end, a large window on the upper floor, and the depth of the tiled roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain facade with multiple windows and the expanse of the tiled roof, no entryway visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a gable end, upper floor window, and visible dormers on the roof.
attribute_entryway_design: Small gabled entryway with a wooden door, tiled overhang, and no visible steps or porch.
